anchor:
- Frontend Engineer with strong expertise in React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS  
- 3+ years of production experience building responsive, high‑performance web applications  
- Comfortable with TypeScript, GraphQL, and modern state‑managment (Redux, Zustand, or React‑Query)  
- Proven ability to write clean, testable code (unit + integration tests with Jest/React‑Testing‑Library)  
- Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and static site generation (Next.js SSG/ISR)  
- Experience with accessibility (WCAG 2.1) and performance optimization (code‑splitting, lazy loading, bundle analysis)

- Remote work acceptable for the first 3 months pending on‑site onboarding; thereafter hybrid 2‑days onsite/week preferred  
- Desired: prior startup experience (scale‑up or early stage) and knowledge of API‑first design  
- Start date: 4‑6 weeks from interview or sooner if experienced; flexibility to negotiate  
- Preferred certifications: AWS Certified Developer – Associate or similar cloud DevOps credential  
- Security clearance: Basic security clearance acceptable, higher clearance preferred for potential future roles.

anchor: We’re scaling fast and looking for an experienced Cybersecurity Engineer (Pen Test & SOC) who can hit the ground running—think 5‑7 years in senior pen‑testing (Burp Suite, Metasploit, OWASP), solid IAM and SOC background (Incident Response, SIEM, XDR), and proven hands‑on with Microsoft Azure AD, Okta, and Active Directory. If you hold a CISSP, CISA, or Guardium certification, that’s a big plus. We’re open to full‑time, remote or hybrid (NYC/WA) placement, and we’re happy to work with C2C contractors for a 12‑month engagement with a quick start date. Let’s chat if you thrive in fast‑paced, high‑impact teams and can bring that expertise to keep our clients’ defenses rock‑solid.

anchor:
- Senior QA Engineer (5+ years of experience) with deep expertise in Selenium WebDriver, Cypress, and JUnit  
- Proven track record building automated test suites for complex web applications (React/Angular/JS)

- Ability to design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipeline integration (Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions)  
- Familiarity with version control (Git), test management (TestRail, Zephyr), and defect tracking (Jira)  
- Comfortable working in Agile/Scrum environments and leading test planning and execution

- Strong communication skills to collaborate with developers, product managers, and stakeholders  
- PMP/ISTQB certification a plus.  
- Remote‑first / hybrid location flexibility (US West Coast preferred, willing to travel 10‑20%)  
- Start date: ASAP, full‑time (non‑C2C)  
- S3, IAM, or elevated SOC 2/ISO 27001 clearance acceptable for compliance projects.

positive.summary: Senior QA Engineer with 6 years of experience building automated test suites for complex web applications using Selenium, Cypress, JUnit, and BDD frameworks. Expert in CI/CD integration and Agile test planning, with strong proficiency in test coverage metrics and communication across cross-functional teams.

work_experience.description: Automated regression and functional testing for Swvl's web platform using Selenium WebDriver, Cypress, and JUnit. Developed test frameworks in Java and JavaScript, wrote BDD scenarios with Cucumber, and integrated tests into GitLab CI pipelines. Maintained test cases in Zephyr and logged defects in Jira. Implemented cross-browser testing on Selenium Grid and improved test coverage from 60% to 70%. Collaborated closely with product and development teams in Agile sprints.

Lead the development of end-to-end automated test suites for Careem's web and mobile web applications built with React and Angular. Designed BDD test scenarios using Cucumber and SpecFlow, orchestrated CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins, GitLab CI, and GitHub Actions. Mentored a team of 4 QA analysts and facilitated Agile ceremonies.

anchor: Senior Embedded Software Engineer – C/C++/RTOS/FPGA (Staff Level) – Full‑Time, Onsite in Austin, TX (Hybrid Remote within 60 miles). 8+ years in embedded systems, deep expertise in C/C++, RTOS (FreeRTOS/FlexRay), VHDL/Verilog FPGA design, low‑level device driver development, DMA/inter‑processor communication, and Ethernet stack. Proven experience in automotive or aerospace domains, with a strong grasp of safety‑critical standards (ISO 26262, DO‑178C). 20 % travel required. Prefer PMP or IEEE certifications and a baseline security clearance. Please submit resume by October 1, 2026.

positive.summary: Staff Embedded Software Engineer with 11+ years of experience designing safety‑critical automotive and aerospace firmware, FPGA accelerators, and Ethernet stacks.

work_experience.description: Led the development of safety‑critical ECU firmware for passenger cars, implementing ISO‑26262 compliant driver stacks, FreeRTOS scheduling, and FlexRay communication. Designed and verified FPGA accelerators in VHDL/Verilog for real‑time sensor data fusion, and developed DMA‑based inter‑processor communication protocols for multi‑core processors.

Responsible for the design of DO‑178C certified flight‑control firmware, integrating complex Ethernet networks, and managing a team of embedded developers. Architected low‑latency real‑time communication between avionics modules using DMA and IPC mechanisms, while ensuring compliance with safety standards.

projects.name: AUTOSAR Ethernet Media Access Layer
projects.description: Designed and implemented a compliant Ethernet MAC layer for an AUTOSAR-based automotive platform, achieving sub‑microsecond latency and low CPU overhead.

projects.description: Developed a high‑throughput FPGA accelerator in VHDL/Verilog for real‑time image filtering in automotive vision systems, interfacing via DMA with an ARM Cortex‑A53 processor.

negative.summary: Entry-level embedded firmware engineer with 1.3 years of experience in STM32 development, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and low-power design on STM32H7/H5 platforms.

work_experience.description: Implemented current‑sense ADC driver for DC‑DC converter monitoring on STM32H750 dual‑core board. Leveraged HAL ADC with DMA, interrupt routines, and FreeRTOS queue integration. Applied MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines, designed Unity unit tests, and used Segger J‑Link for debugging. Maintained GitHub repository with GitLab CI for static analysis and automated flashing using STM32CubeProgrammer.

Developed low‑power UART and DMA drivers for the order collection device, utilizing STM32Cube HAL, CMSIS, FreeRTOS 10.4.x, and ensuring <64‑byte stack per task. Configured STM32H743 board with STM32CubeMX, implemented tickless idle and power‑management features, validated code quality with cppcheck and Unity unit tests, and managed continuous integration via GitLab CI. Debugged with Segger J-Link and flashed with STM32CubeProgrammer.

projects.name: Low‑Power UART Driver
projects.description: UART driver for STM32H743 with FreeRTOS integration, optimized for <64‑byte stack per task, includes interrupt and DMA handling.

projects.description: ADC driver with DMA for DC‑DC monitoring on STM32H750, integrated with FreeRTOS queue and MISRA‑C compliant.
